yatagan.name: Yatagan
yatagan.summary: A yatagan is an Ottoman and Balkan short sword or long knife with a forward-curved single-edged blade, often richly decorated and commonly fitted with bone, horn, or ivory grip scales that can flare at the end.

thumb_ring.name: Thumb ring
thumb_ring.summary: A thumb ring is an archery accessory worn on the thumb to protect it and help draw a bowstring, especially in traditions using a thumb draw. It is not itself a weapon, but it is a weapon-adjacent artifact commonly displayed with bows.

tachi.name: Tachi
tachi.summary: A tachi is a Japanese curved sword that predates the katana and is traditionally associated with edge-down suspension. It is often longer and more curved than a katana, with Japanese fittings and a wrapped handle.

odachi.name: Odachi
odachi.summary: An odachi, also called nodachi in some contexts, is a very large Japanese sword. It is much longer than a typical katana and usually has a curved single-edged blade with a long wrapped grip.

wakizashi.name: Wakizashi
wakizashi.summary: A wakizashi is a short Japanese companion sword, longer than a tanto but shorter than a katana, usually with a curved single-edged blade and traditional wrapped grip.

tanto.name: Tanto
tanto.summary: A tanto is a short Japanese dagger or knife-like blade, typically much shorter than a wakizashi and often fitted in traditional Japanese mountings.
